Don't waste your money trying to fix crepey skin until you see this.

Does anything really work to reverse thin crepe skin?

Well, I needed to find out for myself.

So, I tested all three products for 30 days straight.

So, you don't have to waste your time and money on things that don't deliver.

Now, in January, I started with this high-end retinol body lotion and it had beauty editors buzzing.

But, after religiously applying it every night for a month, there was literally almost no improvement.

My skin was still thin and wrinkled, especially on my upper arms.

So, in February, I moved on to this popular serum that was all over TikTok and it was $65 for a small bottle.

The influencers were saying it instantly plumps skin and eliminates that texture.

So, I followed the directions perfectly and yes, my skin felt more hydrated when the product was on.

But, the paper-thin texture and those fine crepey lines were still completely visible.

So, I classify that as temporarily moisturizing, but completely ineffective for actual crepey skin.

Then came March and it was time to try Dr. J's formula and Dr. J explained that her formula works different because it doesn't just hydrate the surface.

It actually helps restore the skin density and elasticity from within.

So, of course, I'll admit you know I wasn't sure.

And by day seven, I noticed the crepey skin on my chest started to look smoother and less wrinkled.

And by the end of 30 days, my best friend of three decades actually grabbed my arm during lunch and said, what did you do to your skin?

It's amazing.

I need to know your secret.

That's when I knew it was not just wishful thinking.

So, if you're tired of hiding your arms and leg and chest because of that embarrassing crepey skin, this Dr. J formula works.

And I promise you, you'll be amazed that that thin tissue paper skin will transform into the smooth youthful skin that you thought was gone forever.

When I tell my patients to avoid moisturizing their paper-thin skin, they look at me like I'm crazy.

However, after I share with them the truth behind why this good skin habit makes their crepey skin even worse, they throw away their moisturizers and never touch them again.

See, our skin knows how to self-moisturize.

In fact, your skin has what's called natural moisturizing factors all over your body, also known as NMFs.

Think of them as automatic water sprinklers in your front yard.

They go off whenever your skin needs moisture, keeping it hydrated and plump.

Yet, as we age, NMFs start to malfunction, and over time, they go out of service.

So, the skin becomes dry, weak, and flaky.

That's why you might find yourself reapplying moisturizing creams often, and maybe even look to switch a moisturizer with a stronger effect.

I know so many women who almost completely ignore the skin below their faces.

Women like Lisa, who's 66 and looks 46 at first glance, has paper-thin grandma skin on her hands that reveal her true age.

Then there's Julie, who could pass for a 50-something in her 60s if it wasn't for the crepey neck and cleavage.

The thing is, the skin around these delicate areas is often on display.

We don't pay nearly enough attention to it, though.

We let it betray us and give away our real age.

However, this can be fixed quickly.

Just one trick, and you'll see any grandma-looking skin could be optional.

Hi, I'm Dr. Manjula Jagasothi, better known as Dr. J to my patients.

You may have seen my skincare tips online in magazines like Allure, Cosmopolitan, or Bazaar.

I use this solution myself, and it's become a favorite among my patients, family members, and girlfriends.

So, here's the three things you need to feed your skin to stop fibroblast failure.

First, a healthy dose of sugar.

Not the bad-for-you sugar found in soda and candy, but a beauty- supporting sugar called ribose.

A study published in the Clinical Cosmetic and Investigational Dermatology Journal found that ribose supports healthy fibroblast activity.

In this study, the ladies who used ribose saw wrinkles shrink by up to 12% in just 14 days, which is like rolling the clock five to seven years.

Plus, ribose limits the breakdown of skin tissues and prevents new wrinkles from appearing, meaning the longer you use a ribose-infused serum, the longer your results will last.

The second thing you need to feed your skin is fractionated coconut oil.

Unlike extra virgin, fractionated coconut oil penetrates the deeper layers of your skin and improves moisturization, presumably because it activates natural NMFs for better hydration.

Additionally, the American Journal of Clinical Dermatology found it locks in moisture and prevents water loss, which is key if you want to make crepey, creased skin disappear.

It's the perfect companion to ribose, and when you combine these two youth boosters with grapeseed oil, the third and final fibroblast fixer, you'll get even better results.

Researchers have found grapeseed oil improves the skin's softness and bounce back because its antioxidants help stimulate collagen production, which is crucial since fibroblasts have been decreasing your skin's collagen for years.

Research also shows it can even out your skin tone and reduce problems like dark spots.

Together, these three ingredients, ribose, fractionated coconut oil, and grapeseed oil, are all you need to stop fibroblast failure, restore natural moisturizing factors, and free yourself from unflattering crepey skin around the upper arm, the legs, thighs, and knees, cleavage, and abdomen.
